title: Pentaphalangium latissimum
taxonomy: Pentaphalangium latissimum is classified within the family Apiaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, including Apioideae, which is the subfamily that Pentaphalangium latissimum belongs to. Within the subfamily Apioideae, Pentaphalangium latissimum is part of the tribe Oenantheae. The tribe Oenantheae contains a variety of species, many of which are native to Asia and Europe. Pentaphalangium latissimum is native to the Himalayas and surrounding regions.
uses: Pentaphalangium latissimum, a plant native to China, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. The plant's extracts have been studied for their ability to reduce inflammation and protect against oxidative stress.
